events.title: Workshop: Support your People
events.description: Learn about what you might say and do if someone shares that they've experienced violence. Mon 4/27 2:30-3:30pm | New Africa House Room 213| April is Sexual Assault Awareness Month (SAAM).

events.title: AI Is Changing Everything—but What Is Everything? A Conversation with Kevin Roose
events.description: Artificial intelligence is transforming how we work, learn and live, but what does that really mean for society? Join the Manning College of Information and Computer Sciences for a special conversation with The New York Times technology columnist and bestselling author Kevin Roose, one of the leading voices covering the rapid evolution of AI. In discussion with Ethan Zuckerman, associate professor of public policy, communication, and information, Roose will explore how advances in artificial intelligence are reshaping not only technical systems, but the social, political, economic, and cultura

events.title: Latinx Coffee Hour
events.description: Creating a safe space for students to share their culture, experiences, and make new connections. Come for coffee, snacks, & good conversation!

events.title: America First, Japan First: Trump, Takaichi and the Nationalist Turn on Economic Security
events.description: As nationalist politics reshape economic policy on both sides of the Pacific, how are the United States and Japan navigating their partnership — and what tensions lie beneath the surface? Join us for a timely online lecture with Eriko Stronach ('12), a UMass Amherst Japanese program alumna, as she examines the emerging relationship between the Trump administration and Japanese politician Sanae Takaichi, and what it means for bilateral economic security cooperation. About the Speaker Eriko Stronach is a Senior Associate at The Asia Group in Washington, DC, where she helps clients navigate Japan

events.title: Tay Gavin Erickson Lecture Series: Dr. Y Alicia Hong
events.description: Tay Gavin Erickson Lecture Series Dr. Y Alicia Hong; George Mason University From Information to Intelligence: Health Communication in a Digital World Register Here! Health communication has shifted from helping people find information to supporting real-time, personalized, and increasingly intelligent interactions. This talk traces that evolution across internet use, patient-provider communication, digital interventions, and the rise of AI. It offers insights into what these changes mean for research, practice, and the future of health communication in a digital world. About Dr. Hong Dr. Alic

events.title: Drawing Curious Spaces - Drawing Outside - Durfee Conservatory / Permaculture Garden
events.description: The Drawing Curious Spaces Series is comprised of a number of informal gatherings at some quirky and less know spaces on campus focused on slow seeing and drawing. No registration necessary; drop-in style means you come for what you can. Each gathering is a stand-alone event. No drawing experience necessary; each attendee gets use of a drawing kit. This project is funded by the Office of Equity and Inclusion.

events.title: MS in Business Analytics Virtual Information Session
events.description: Join to learn more about the Isenberg MS in Business Analytics program. The session will cover FAQ's about the admissions process, curriculum, and career outcomes. We will discuss both the online model as well as the on-campus Amherst and Newton options. Register today!

events.title: Chemical Engineering for the Common Good: Ideas for Turning “Unrecyclable” Waste into Valuable Products
events.description: Have you ever wondered what really happens to a chip bag after you toss it? Have you thought about the hidden price we pay to keep those chips perfectly crispy? Join us to uncover the fate of multilayer plastic packaging and see how senior UMass Amherst Chemical Engineering students are designing innovative re/upcycling processes to turn multilayer plastics into valuable products. Presented in partnership with the Department of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering.

events.title: BPQC Meeting: Queerness in Fan Fiction
events.description: The Bi/Pan/Queer Collective (BPQC), a social group for bi, pan, queer, and sexually fluid students, will be discussing queerness in fan fiction at its April 28th meeting. Open to all students.

events.title: Libraries' Page to Screen Club
events.description: Drop in to the Libraries' multimedia book club, where we read a book and then watch the screen adaptation! We meet for several weeks to talk about the book, then watch the screen adaptation together and compare. All sessions are 7-8:30 p.m. except for screenings, which are 7-9:30 p.m. The breakdown for what pages/sections to read will be determined at a later planning session. Little Women, Louisa May Alcott February 3 February 10 February 17 February 24 March 3 - Screening Stories of Your Life and Others, Ted Chiang March 10 March 17 - No meeting; spring vacation March 24 - Screening Jurassic

events.title: Anthropology at Work
events.description: Faculty will open their lab spaces to showcase their work, with hands on activities and demos. Labs are located across Machmer Hall (Anthropology Department). Upon arriving at Machmer, guests will find host students who will point them to specific labs. Human Evolution/Noli Lab (Brigitte Holt, Machmer Hall W14, 9AM-12PM): Life in an Italian Medieval village on the sea We will open “windows” into the past to show how bioarcheological methods help us reconstruct the lives of the people who once lived in Noli, a small medieval coastal village.
